phases.name: Expand the seeded vocabulary to the common software philosophies

steps.name: seed the common software philosophies (process, design, architecture/ops) into seeds.json
steps.description: The slice-01 embedded seed vocabulary shipped 12 records skewed toward language/build/data properties. Users expect the philosophies that appear in most software projects (agile, xp, scrum, devops, solid, dry, kiss, clean-architecture, microservices, 12-factor, IaC, ...). Add ~50 curated org.openlore.philosophy records to crates/lexicon/src/seeds.json spanning three categories — process methodologies, design principles, architecture/ops patterns — each with a rich 2-sentence description, aliases, and a canonical seeAlso URL. No production code changes: seeds() reads the same include_str! file; every new record is validated by the existing generalizing property tests over prop::sample::select(seeds()).
criteria: Every new seed validates through validate_philosophy_json (non-blank name+description; aliases/seeAlso arrays of strings)
GUARDRAIL every existing property test (find_resolves_every_seed_by_name_and_object_id, resolve_object_advisory_*, equivalence_class_*, find_is_total_and_sound) stays green over the widened set
GUARDRAIL seeds_tests::hard_pinned_names_are_all_present + ships_at_least_ten_seeds + seed_names_have_distinct_object_ids stay green (original 12 preserved)
OWNED every_seed_name_and_alias_normalizes_uniquely (no normalized name/alias collision — resolvers return the FIRST match)
OWNED seed_vocabulary_covers_common_software_philosophies (>= 60 records; canonical anchors across the three categories resolve by name AND object-id)
steps.implementation_notes: Pure data slice: the acceptance net already exists as generalizing property tests parameterized on prop::sample::select(seeds()), so widening seeds.json exercises every new record automatically. The ONE invariant a bare data edit can violate is token uniqueness: find/resolve_object_advisory return the first normalized match, so two seeds whose names OR aliases normalize to the same token would silently mis-resolve (and break find_resolves_every_seed_by_name_and_object_id). Pinned by the new every_seed_name_and_alias_normalizes_uniquely test, which fails naming the offending token. No crate/route/dependency change — check-arch stays at 21 members. Demo: openlore philosophy list.

validation.reviewer: orchestrator (/nw:new wizard, user-directed scope) + automated quality gate (cargo test -p lexicon)
validation.review_verdict: APPROVED — single-step pure-data slice. Existing generalizing property tests are the acceptance net; 2 owned pins (uniqueness invariant + coverage floor) added. All 47 lexicon tests green over the 64-record set; check-arch OK (21). No production logic changed.
